text.title: C# .NET Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules grounding clean-architecture + CQRS + rich-domain patterns on .NET 8.

rule.statement: Solution MUST have four projects with one-way reference flow: `Domain` (no deps) ← `Application` ← `Infrastructure` ← `Web`. `Domain` MUST NOT reference `Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore`, `Microsoft.AspNetCore.*`, or any external SDK.

rationale.source: Clean Architecture (Robert C. Martin) + Jason Taylor .NET template

rule.rationale: One-way dependency keeps Domain pure and testable. Project-reference graph validates at csproj parse — no runtime cost.

rule.statement: Each command/query MUST be a `record` implementing `IRequest<TResponse>` with exactly one `IRequestHandler` in `Application/`. No generic `IService` classes that mix multiple operations.

rationale.source: MediatR docs + Jimmy Bogard CQRS posts

rule.rationale: One-handler-per-message preserves CQRS read/write separation and makes pipeline behaviours (logging, validation, transactions) attachable in one place.

rule.statement: Entities/aggregates MUST have no public setters. Behaviour MUST live on the aggregate as intention-revealing methods (`order.Ship()`, not `order.Status = "shipped"`). State changes go through methods that enforce invariants.
rule.rationale: Public setters allow callers to bypass invariants; the aggregate cannot enforce business rules consistently.

rule.statement: Request DTOs MUST carry FluentValidation `AbstractValidator<T>` registered in DI; the MediatR validation pipeline behaviour MUST run before the handler. No `if (req.Foo == null) throw...` scattered in handlers.
rule.rationale: Edge-validated requests fail fast with `ValidationException` mapped to 400 `ProblemDetails`. Centralized rules are reusable and testable in isolation.

rule.statement: Web layer MUST NOT return Domain entities. Responses MUST be `record` DTOs defined in `Application/` (or `Web/Contracts/`). Entities never appear in controller method signatures, MediatR responses, or Swagger schemas.
rule.rationale: Returning entities couples persistence shape to the public contract; adding a column or navigation breaks every client.
